apiErrors.bad_request: The request could not be processed.
apiErrors.invalid_json: The request data is invalid. Please refresh and try again.
apiErrors.invalid_request_body: The request data is invalid. Please check your input and try again.
apiErrors.validation_failed: Some required information is missing or invalid.
apiErrors.forbidden: You do not have permission to perform this action.
apiErrors.not_found: The requested resource was not found.
apiErrors.conflict: This action conflicts with the current state. Please refresh and try again.
apiErrors.service_unavailable: The service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.
apiErrors.upstream_error: The external service returned an error. Please check the connection and try again.
apiErrors.internal_error: Something went wrong on the server. Please try again later.
apiErrors.agent_access_required: You do not have access to this agent.
apiErrors.agent_already_exists: An agent with this name already exists.
apiErrors.invalid_credentials: The email or password is incorrect.
apiErrors.invitation_not_found: The invitation was not found or has expired.
apiErrors.project_access_required: You do not have access to this project.
apiErrors.user_already_exists: A user with this email or username already exists.
apiErrors.workspace_access_required: You do not have access to this workspace.
apiErrors.workspace_database_unavailable: The workspace database is unavailable. Please try again later.
apiErrors.connection_access_required: You do not have access to this connection.
apiErrors.connection_grant_not_found: The connection grant was not found.
apiErrors.provider_not_found: The connector provider was not found.
apiErrors.runtime_agent_token_required: A valid runtime agent token is required.
apiErrors.runtime_connection_not_granted: This connection is not granted to the agent.
apiErrors.runtime_capability_required: The runtime token does not allow this action.
apiErrors.unsupported_auth_type: This authentication type is not supported.
apiErrors.unsupported_provider: This connector provider is not supported.
apiErrors.agent_not_running: The agent is not currently running.
apiErrors.cron_not_found: The cron job was not found.
apiErrors.heartbeat_not_found: The heartbeat configuration was not found.
apiErrors.process_not_found: The agent process could not be found.
apiErrors.scheduler_conflict: The scheduler is already running or conflicts with the current state.
apiErrors.scheduler_wakeup_failed: Failed to wake up the agent.
apiErrors.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again.

workspaceOnboarding.title: Create your workspace
workspaceOnboarding.subtitle: Start with a shared space for your agent team.
workspaceOnboarding.name: Workspace name

connections.title: External Tools
connections.subtitle: Configure third-party credentials so workspace agents can use them safely through Multigent.
connections.emptyHint: Configure tool credentials so agents can safely access third-party platforms.
connections.disconnectConnectionConfirm: Disconnect {{name}}? Agents will no longer be able to use this tool.
connections.oauthClientConfigsHint: Configure provider OAuth apps once per workspace. Client secrets stay server-side.
connections.connectedReadonlyHint: Connected tool credentials are not edited in place. Disconnect and configure again to replace credentials.
connections.keepCurrentSecret: Leave blank to keep current secret
connections.oauthStartHint: Saving will start the provider OAuth flow. The callback creates this connection without exposing tokens to the browser.
connections.keepCurrentValue: Leave blank to keep current value
connections.credentialsHiddenHint: Existing credential values are hidden. Fill only the fields you want to replace; leave all credential fields blank to keep the current secret.
connections.accountProfileHint: Safe identity and authorization metadata shown to humans and agent runtimes. Do not place secrets here.
connections.automaticHealthChecksHint: Multigent periodically validates this connection without exposing secrets to agents.
connections.runtimeActionPolicyHint: Optional allow/block rules for agent HTTP action proxy calls. Endpoint patterns support exact paths,
connections.personalGrantHint: Personal connections can only be granted by their owner to that owner or agents they can operate.
connections.workspaceGrantHint: Grant this workspace connection to a workspace, project, agent, or user.
connections.externalToolDefaultDescription: Connect this external tool so agents can call it safely through Multigent.
connections.noConnectionsForTool: No connections yet. Create a workspace or personal connection.
connections.oauthNotConfiguredAdmin: This tool supports OAuth. Configure an OAuth app to let users authorize personal accounts.
connections.oauthHiddenForUsers: OAuth for this tool has not been enabled by an admin; only available static credential methods are shown.
connections.deviceAuthHint: Open the authorization page or scan the QR code. Multigent will save the usable credential automatically.
connections.authorizationConnected: Authorization completed and the connection was created.

guideBodies.personal_access_token: Create a personal access token from the provider's account or developer settings. Prefer least-privilege scopes and add write permissions only when an agent workflow requires them.
guideBodies.api_key: Create a dedicated API key for Multigent in the provider's developer console, scoped to the capabilities this workspace needs.
guideBodies.app_credential: Create an internal app in the provider console, enable the required APIs, publish or install it, then copy the App ID and App Secret.
guideBodies.internal_integration_token: Create an internal integration, copy its token, and explicitly grant it access to the pages, databases, or resources agents should use.
guideBodies.api_token: Create an API token from personal or developer settings. Prefer a dedicated account or least-privilege token.
guideBodies.auth_token: Create an auth token from account or organization settings. Start with read-only scopes and add write access only when needed.
guideBodies.access_token: Create an access token from account settings. Prefer team-scoped or project-scoped tokens over broad personal tokens.
guideBodies.webhook_credential: Create a custom bot or webhook, then copy its access token or webhook URL. Fill the signing secret too if signature validation is enabled.
guideBodies.mcp_gateway: Optional. Add an MCP-compatible HTTP endpoint so agents can use this tool through the unified gateway; built-in actions still work without it.
guideBodies.oauth_app: A workspace admin must configure this provider's OAuth app first. Use least-privilege scopes and a dedicated production OAuth app.

providerDescriptions.github: Connect repositories, issues, pull requests, releases, and engineering collaboration context.
providerDescriptions.gitlab: Connect GitLab repositories, merge requests, issues, and CI/CD context.
providerDescriptions.gitee: Connect Gitee repositories, issues, pull requests, and domestic code hosting workflows.
providerDescriptions.feishu: Connect Feishu OpenAPI for docs, wiki, messaging, and collaboration workflows.
providerDescriptions.lark: Connect Lark OpenAPI for global docs, messaging, and collaboration workflows.
providerDescriptions.linear: Connect Linear issues, projects, cycles, and engineering planning context.
providerDescriptions.jira: Connect Jira issues, epics, projects, and enterprise development workflows.
providerDescriptions.notion: Connect Notion pages, databases, knowledge bases, and lightweight product docs.
providerDescriptions.slack: Connect Slack channels, messages, threads, and team notification workflows.
providerDescriptions.dingtalk_bot: Connect DingTalk group bots for notifications and workflow updates.
providerDescriptions.gmail: Connect Gmail for email search, triage, and draft workflows.
providerDescriptions.google_drive: Connect Google Drive files, folders, attachments, and knowledge sources.
providerDescriptions.google_docs: Connect Google Docs documents for readable and writable knowledge artifacts.
providerDescriptions.google_sheets: Connect Google Sheets for structured data, operations tables, and reporting.
providerDescriptions.google_calendar: Connect Google Calendar for schedules, meetings, and reminders.
providerDescriptions.figma: Connect Figma files, components, comments, and design handoff context.
providerDescriptions.airtable: Connect Airtable bases for operations tables, CRM data, and internal workflows.
providerDescriptions.asana: Connect Asana tasks, projects, sections, and non-engineering project workflows.
providerDescriptions.clickup: Connect ClickUp tasks, lists, spaces, and cross-functional work tracking.
providerDescriptions.sentry: Connect Sentry issues, error events, releases, and triage signals.
providerDescriptions.vercel: Connect Vercel projects, deployments, checks, and release status.
providerDescriptions.exa: Connect Exa neural search for research, source discovery, and agent browsing workflows.
providerDescriptions.brave_search: Connect Brave Search API for independent web search and lightweight source discovery.

workflows.workspaceSubtitle: Inspect and configure workspace-wide workflow whiteboards that tasks can use.
workflows.emptyHint: Create workflows so tasks can start workflow runs from them.
workflows.subtitle: Define and inspect task workflows with a whiteboard-style graph.
workflows.taskWorkflowHint: When selected, creating the task starts a workflow run.
workflows.actorBindingsRequired: Assign an actor for every workflow role.
workflows.createWorkflowHint: Start from a blank workflow or create from a system template. Templates are rendered in the current language once; the created workflow is then a normal editable workflow.
workflows.blankWorkflowHint: Start with one node and design your own steps and branches.
workflows.fromTemplateHint: Use a built-in multilingual workflow template, then customize it freely.
workflows.confirmDeleteWorkflow: Delete workflow "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
workflows.unsavedDescription: Leaving this page will discard your workflow changes. Are you sure you want to leave?
workflows.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe when this workflow should be used.

projectSettings.subtitle: Project configuration and prompts.
projectSettings.name: Name
projectSettings.deleteProjectDesc: Delete this project, its agents, prompts, and project-local files from the current workspace.
projectSettings.confirmDeleteProject: Delete project "{{name}}"? This will also delete its agents and cannot be undone.

sidebar.footer: Teams hold your roster; projects are where tasks and messages run. Data will connect to multigent later.

settings.title: Workspace Settings
settings.intro: Workspace administration, access control, providers, and runtime environment configuration.
settings.themeHint: Tip: use the sun / moon / monitor icon in the top bar to cycle light → dark → system.
settings.externalToolOAuthIntro: Admins configure provider OAuth clients here. External Tools only shows OAuth authorization to users after a client is configured.
settings.keepCurrentSecret: Leave blank to keep current secret

account.title: Account Settings
account.intro: Personal preferences and password for your current browser session.
account.profileDescription: Set the name and avatar shown to teammates and agents in Multigent.
account.cropAvatarHint: Adjust the zoom and position, then save your profile.
account.displayNameDescription: This name appears in member lists, messages, and audit logs.
account.preferencesDescription: These preferences only affect the current user and device.
account.languageDescription: Choose the display language for this browser.
account.appearanceDescription: Set the interface theme used on this device.
account.passwordDescription: Update your password for the current Multigent account.
account.currentPasswordDescription: Enter your current password to confirm it is you.
account.newPasswordDescription: Use a new password with at least 6 characters.
account.confirmPasswordDescription: Enter the new password again to avoid typos.

overview.subtitle: Cross-project snapshot: pending confirmations, in-flight work, and recent runs. Humans and agents use the same semantics; drill into a project for execution detail.
overview.telemetryHint: Telemetry summary; unavailable if the DB is not configured.
overview.telemetryNoDb: Telemetry database not available or no data yet.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.subtitle: Current workspace profile, ownership metadata, and local runtime boundary.
workspace.name: Name
workspace.switchHint: This local API instance is bound to one workspace root. SaaS-style creation and switching will be handled by the future workspace registry/control plane.

inbox.subtitle: Project-scoped thread: confirmations, forwards, and unread items. Each row shows from and assignee (human or agent).
inbox.emptyBody: API not connected yet. Actions will align with CLI inbox, filtered by this project.

tasks.subtitle: Task queue for this project—status, filters, assign to people or agents equally.
tasks.emptyBody: Connect the workspace API to list multigent tasks for this project.
tasks.confirmDelete: Delete this task? This cannot be undone.
tasks.confirmBatchDelete: Delete {{count}} selected task(s)? This cannot be undone.
tasks.assignHint: Leave default to assign to the selected agent, or choose human for manual handling.
tasks.noRunRecord: No execution record found for this task.
tasks.summaryPlaceholder: Describe the outcome or leave a note…
tasks.willNotifyCreator: A notification will be sent to {{creator}} upon saving.
tasks.parentTaskHint: Pick a parent to create a sub-task

projects.listSubtitle: Projects are where execution happens: open one to manage tasks, messages, and on-project members. Teams maintain the roster; projects assign members.
projects.createHint: Create an empty project. You can add members, tasks, schedules, and agents next.
projects.name: Project name
projects.demoHintBody: Use “demo” to preview the project sidebar: Tasks, Messages, Members—all scoped to one project ID until the API is wired.
projects.emptyBody: Connect the workspace API to list real projects from disk.

members.subtitle: Project members, including humans and agents.
members.confirmFire: Remove member "{{name}}" from the project? Their working directory will be archived.
members.addMemberDesc: Add a human member or agent to project "{{project}}".
members.runtimeConnectionsHelp: External tool accounts and credentials this agent can use at runtime. Workspace-level tools are available to workspace agents by default.
members.noRuntimeConnections: No available tool connections. Configure a workspace tool on the external tools page first.

skill.catalogHint: All skills available in this workspace. Expand to view and edit SKILL.md.

skill.name: Skill name
skill.contentPlaceholder:
# Skill: ...

Describe when to use it, the workflow, constraints, and examples.
skill.registryHint: Later this can connect to installers such as npx skills and install external skills into this workspace.

teams.subtitle: Maintain teams, roles, and agent roster. Projects pull people/agents from here when you staff a delivery.
teams.placeholderBody: Next: list teams from agency.yaml / store, open a team to see members, sync agents, and link projects that use this team.
teams.detailPlaceholderBody: Roster, skills, and add-member actions will live here; projects reference this team for staffing.
teams.createTeamDesc: Create a blank functional team or start from a reusable role template.
teams.blankTeamDesc: Create a team with no predefined roles. You can add roles later.
teams.teamDescPlaceholder: Short description of what this team is responsible for

teams.confirmDeleteTeam: Delete team "{{name}}" and all its roles? This cannot be undone.

product-discovery.name: Product Discovery Team
product-discovery.description: Turns market signals, user problems, and business goals into validated roadmap scope.

software-delivery.name: Software Delivery Team
software-delivery.description: Cross-functional product engineering team for shipping web/SaaS features with product, design, frontend, backend, QA, and review coverage.

growth-business.name: Growth & Business Team
growth-business.description: Commercial team for pipeline, partnerships, pricing, campaigns, CRM hygiene, and revenue experiments.

customer-operations.name: Customer Operations Team
customer-operations.description: Support, onboarding, feedback loops, incident communication, and knowledge base maintenance.

agent-enablement.name: Agent Enablement Team
agent-enablement.description: Internal platform team for agent context, skills, prompts, tool access, evaluation, and workflow improvement.

data-insights.name: Data & Insights Team
data-insights.description: Analytics team for metrics, dashboards, experiment analysis, research synthesis, and decision support.

product-strategist.name: Product Strategist
product-strategist.description: Owns product direction, opportunity framing, roadmap trade-offs, and strategic alignment.

user-researcher.name: User Researcher
user-researcher.description: Owns customer interviews, usability findings, evidence quality, and insight synthesis.

product-analyst.name: Product Analyst
product-analyst.description: Owns product metrics, funnel analysis, cohort trends, and experiment readouts.

prd-writer.name: PRD Writer
prd-writer.description: Turns product decisions into clear PRDs, acceptance criteria, non-goals, and launch notes.

product-manager.name: Product Manager
product-manager.description: Owns problem framing, scope, acceptance criteria, rollout, and stakeholder alignment.

ui-designer.name: UI Designer
ui-designer.description: Owns user flows, interface structure, visual consistency, and design QA.

frontend-developer.name: Frontend Developer
frontend-developer.description: Owns client implementation, state, accessibility, responsiveness, and frontend integration.

backend-developer.name: Backend Developer
backend-developer.description: Owns APIs, data model, integrations, permissions, reliability, and backend tests.

qa-engineer.name: QA Engineer
qa-engineer.description: Owns test strategy, acceptance validation, regression risk, and release confidence.

code-reviewer.name: Code Reviewer
code-reviewer.description: Reviews changes for correctness, maintainability, security, performance, and test gaps.

growth-marketer.name: Growth Marketer
growth-marketer.description: Owns acquisition experiments, campaign planning, messaging tests, and channel performance.

sales-operator.name: Sales Operator
sales-operator.description: Owns CRM hygiene, lead qualification, follow-ups, pipeline reporting, and sales handoffs.

partnership-manager.name: Partnership Manager
partnership-manager.description: Owns partner research, outreach plans, collaboration proposals, and relationship tracking.

revenue-analyst.name: Revenue Analyst
revenue-analyst.description: Owns pricing analysis, revenue metrics, conversion tracking, and business experiment readouts.

support-specialist.name: Support Specialist
support-specialist.description: Owns support triage, customer replies, issue classification, and escalation quality.

onboarding-specialist.name: Onboarding Specialist
onboarding-specialist.description: Owns customer onboarding flows, setup checklists, adoption blockers, and early success signals.

knowledge-manager.name: Knowledge Manager
knowledge-manager.description: Owns help center content, internal runbooks, FAQ quality, and documentation freshness.

incident-communicator.name: Incident Communicator
incident-communicator.description: Owns customer-facing incident updates, status notes, postmortem summaries, and communication timing.

agent-ops-manager.name: Agent Ops Manager
agent-ops-manager.description: Owns agent staffing, responsibilities, wakeup routines, escalation rules, and operating cadence.

prompt-engineer.name: Prompt Engineer
prompt-engineer.description: Owns role prompts, workflow prompts, prompt tests, and prompt versioning.

skill-builder.name: Skill Builder
skill-builder.description: Owns reusable skills, tool wrappers, MCP integrations, and agent-facing documentation.

eval-engineer.name: Eval Engineer
eval-engineer.description: Owns agent evaluation cases, output review rubrics, regression checks, and quality metrics.

data-analyst.name: Data Analyst
data-analyst.description: Owns metric definitions, SQL analysis, dashboard interpretation, and decision notes.

analytics-engineer.name: Analytics Engineer
analytics-engineer.description: Owns data modeling, transformation quality, metric pipelines, and data reliability checks.

experiment-analyst.name: Experiment Analyst
experiment-analyst.description: Owns experiment design review, result analysis, statistical caveats, and recommendation quality.

research-synthesizer.name: Research Synthesizer
research-synthesizer.description: Owns synthesis across qualitative notes, market research, support signals, and product data.

workbench.title: Workbench
workbench.subtitle: Your action items: messages from agents across projects and tasks that need your attention, all in one place.
workbench.quickLinksHint: Right-click a top tab and choose "Add to quick links" to jump back here later.
workbench.emptyMessagesHint: Messages from agents to you will appear here.
workbench.emptyTasksHint: Cross-project task summary, including all active tasks.

api.noMembers: No agents in this project yet.

forms.title: Title
forms.subject: Subject (optional)
forms.toRequired: At least one recipient is required.
forms.needAgentsForTask: No agents in this project yet. Add an agent first.

messages.confirmDelete: Delete this message? This cannot be undone.
messages.markAllHint: Mark unread messages read in this project’s agent mailboxes.
messages.confirmBatchDelete: Delete {{count}} selected message(s)? This cannot be undone.

schedule.subtitle: Per-agent heartbeat and cron schedules. Start the scheduler to begin automated agent wakeups.
schedule.jitterHint: Random delay added before each wakeup to avoid thundering-herd. 0 = no jitter after first cycle.
schedule.cronSessionHint: New each run: starts a fresh session per execution. Persistent: reuse the same session across runs, copy the command to continue directly.
schedule.noActiveAgents: No agents with heartbeat enabled in this project.
schedule.noRuntimeAgents: No agents with heartbeat or event triggers configured in this project.
schedule.periodicWakeupHint: Applied to interval-based heartbeats. Built-in and script conditions can be used together.
schedule.scriptConditionRequired: Enter a script or command, or turn off script condition.
schedule.wakeupConditionHint: Periodic heartbeat wakes when any selected condition passes. Select none to always wake. Event triggers below are independent instant wakeups.
schedule.presetRequireAny: Pass when tasks or messages exist
schedule.triggersHint: When enabled, the agent wakes up immediately on the selected events without waiting for the heartbeat interval
schedule.restartHint: Changes to heartbeat/cron settings take effect after restarting the scheduler.
schedule.configEffectHint: Configuration changes take effect in the next wakeup cycle automatically.
schedule.confirmAbort: Are you sure you want to abort the running agent? This will immediately terminate its process.

session.scopeHint: Cycle: shared within one wakeup. Per-task: fresh per task. Persistent: kept across wakeups.
session.sessionIdHint: Set a specific session ID to resume context from a previous run, or clear to start fresh.
session.runAgentDesc: Run an agent with a custom prompt, or execute its next pending task.
session.promptPlaceholder: Enter the prompt / instructions for the agent…
session.promptHint: If provided, a task will be created and run immediately. If left empty, the next pending task will be executed.

agentChat.title: Chat
agentChat.historyTruncated: History logs are too large and were truncated.
agentChat.empty: No session content to show yet.
agentChat.emptyHint: Send a message to continue through the current agent CLI and session.
agentChat.sendHint: Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a newline. This first version reuses agent run logs.
agentChat.sessionIdPlaceholder: Paste an external or existing Session ID
agentChat.sessionSwitchHint: Paste a Session ID created in another terminal or tool; the next message will resume that session.
agentChat.followingLiveLog: Following this agent's live run log. A run record will be written when it finishes.

provider.title: Model Accounts
provider.desc: Configure shared model accounts once. Agents reuse them without handling API keys or environment variables.
provider.officialHint: Official accounts only need an API key. Multigent writes the right environment variables or config files for Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini, and other CLIs at runtime.
provider.gatewayHint: Third-party or compatible gateways currently target Codex and Claude Code only. They need an endpoint; the default model can be left empty, and each agent can still override the runtime model.
provider.personalFallbackHint: Only workspace admins can create shared accounts. This account will be personal.
provider.apiKeyHint: Multigent stores the key server-side and uses it only in agent sandboxes that pass permission checks.
provider.removeDescription: Agents using this account may fail until another model account is selected.
provider.overrideHint: The selected account provides endpoint and credentials. Agent model can override only the model name.

agentDetail.modelCredentialsHint: Choose the agent CLI, runtime model, and model account. Available models depend on the selected credential.
agentDetail.connectAndChatHint: Talk from Web Chat, or connect the agent to Feishu, Lark, and future collaboration channels.
agentDetail.capabilitiesHint: Review the external capabilities this agent can use at runtime and the skills inherited from its team and role.
agentDetail.promptContextHint: Tune this agent's behavior prompt and review the complete merged context it actually receives.
agentDetail.toolsAndSkillsHint: Review the external capabilities this agent can use at runtime and the skills inherited from its team and role.
agentDetail.instructionsHint: Tune this agent's behavior prompt and review the complete merged context it actually receives.
agentDetail.skillsHint: These skills usually come from team and role configuration and are injected into the agent context.
agentDetail.selectToolConnection: Select a tool connection to enable
agentDetail.contextHint: Review the complete merged context the agent receives, including prompt, role, project, and skill content.

runs.subtitle: Run reliability, duration, and token usage for this project’s agents.
runs.noDbBody: Configure and populate the agent_runs database to see this data.
runs.logNotFound: Log file not found or cleaned up
runs.logTruncated: Log truncated (too large to display in full).

agentChannels.title: Collaboration Channels
agentChannels.subtitle: Connect this agent to workplace communication tools so people can intervene from their existing workflow.
agentChannels.connectHint: Scan to create or bind an app bot for this agent.
agentChannels.chatPending: Chat not bound yet. The first addressed message will bind it.
agentChannels.scanHint: Scan with {{provider}} and approve the app bot connection.
agentChannels.connectedReady: {{provider}} is connected. You can now message the bot to work with this agent.
agentChannels.securityHint: Configure this callback URL in the Feishu/Lark developer console. Verification Token is checked on message events; Encrypt Key decrypts encrypted Feishu/Lark events.
agentChannels.keepExisting: Leave empty to keep unchanged; enter a new value to update
agentChannels.sessionIdle: Idle. Collaboration can start from Web or Feishu/Lark.

sandbox.title: Runtime Environment
sandbox.agentCliHint: Installed and upgraded outside the base image
sandbox.addDirsHint: Directories made available to the runtime. Production providers should materialize per-run copies instead of sharing writable host paths.
